ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for tater tots; closest parent 'potato product (food)' FOODON_03301710 is used as the nearest defensible ancestor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (Potato products) is the closest parent category; no specific tater tots code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for tater tots or equivalent fried potato snack product.

allergens: may contain gluten depending on seasoning

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of tater tots is approximately 85 grams, equivalent to about 9 to 10 individual tots, as commonly served as a snack or side dish.

nutrient_source.title: Potatoes, tater tots, frozen, prepared (USDA FoodData Central, FDC ID 170030)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for frozen prepared tater tots (FDC ID 170030) used as the primary nutrient reference. Values represent the generic fried/prepared tater tot product. The Potato Corner 'Mega Tater Tots' sold in Singapore is a commercial variant; exact branded nutrition data is not available in an approved source, so USDA SR Legacy values for the closest matching product are used. Confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — fried potato / potato products
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for tater tots in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 75 is estimated by analogy with fried potato products (french fries, hash browns) reported in Atkinson FS et al. (2021) International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values, Diabetes Care. Fried potato products consistently show GI in the range 70–80 (high GI). Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (75 × 22.5) / 100 ≈ 16.9, where carbohydrate per 85 g serving = 26.5 × 0.85 = 22.5 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (352 mg per 100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit tater tot consumption due to the high sodium content of approximately 352 mg per 100 g; a single 85 g serving contributes roughly 299 mg sodium, which represents about 13% of the 2,300 mg daily limit recommended by major cardiovascular guidelines.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (~75) and significant carbohydrate load
type2_diabetes.guidance: Tater tots carry a high glycemic index of approximately 75 and a glycemic load of about 16.9 per standard serving, making them likely to cause rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume them only occasionally and in small portions, ideally paired with protein or fibre to blunt the glycemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate total fat with saturated fat and high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Tater tots contain approximately 9.5 g fat and 1.6 g saturated fat per 100 g alongside high sodium; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit intake as the combination of saturated fat and sodium contributes to atherogenic and hypertensive risk, and deep-frying may introduce additional oxidised lipids depending on the oil used.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Tater tots are small, cylindrical or oval-shaped fried potato snacks made from grated or shredded potato that is formed into bite-sized pieces and deep-fried until golden and crispy on the outside while remaining soft and fluffy on the inside. Originating in the United States and popularised by the Ore-Ida brand in the 1950s, tater tots have become a globally recognised fast-food and snack item. In Singapore, they are served by chains such as Potato Corner, where they are offered in larger 'Mega' portions with a choice of flavoured seasonings including cheese, sour cream, and barbecue. Nutritionally, tater tots are an energy-dense snack providing approximately 197 kcal per 100 g, with carbohydrates as the dominant macronutrient at 26.5 g per 100 g, fat at 9.5 g per 100 g from the frying process, and modest protein at 2.3 g per 100 g. Sodium content is notable at 352 mg per 100 g, which is clinically relevant for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 75 (high), consistent with other fried potato products, making portion control important for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or insulin resistance. Tater tots are generally vegetarian and vegan-friendly in their plain form, though seasoning variants may contain dairy or other allergens. They are classified as an ultra-processed food due to the industrial shaping, frying, and seasoning processes involved.
common_user_queries: How many calories are in tater tots?
What is the glycemic index of tater tots?
How much sodium is in tater tots?
What are tater tots made of?
Potato Corner Mega Tater Tots nutrition
Tater tots vs french fries nutrition

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for the closest matching generic frozen prepared tater tots product (FDC ID 170030). The Potato Corner 'Mega Tater Tots' is a commercial branded product sold in Singapore for which no exact nutritional panel is available in an approved primary source. GI is an analogous estimate based on fried potato products from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021). Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 170030, 'Potatoes, tater tots, frozen, prepared') as the closest approved-source match. The Potato Corner Mega Tater Tots product sold in Singapore may differ in exact composition depending on the oil used, seasoning applied, and portion size. 2. trans_fat_g is reported as 0.0; this is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy data for this product category. cholesterol_mg is reported as 0.0; confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy as this is a plant-derived fried product with no animal-fat additions in the base formulation. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is 0.0 (confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy for this product). vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 (naturally zero as this is a plant-based product with no animal-derived ingredients in the base formulation). vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 (confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy; not reported as present in this product). 3. GI of 75 is an analogous estimate based on fried potato products (french fries, hash browns) from Atkinson FS et al. (2021) International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values, Diabetes Care; no direct tater tots GI measurement was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. 4. Seasoning variants (cheese, sour cream, barbecue) offered by Potato Corner will alter the sodium, fat, and calorie profile; values here represent the plain/base tater tot without added seasoning powder. 5. Multilingual local names are not applicable for this food as it is an American-origin Western snack food, not a Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, or Vietnamese dish; the multilingual naming requirement under G6 does not apply. 6. monounsaturated_fat_g (-1 sentinel not used; value 4.1 sourced from USDA SR Legacy). polyunsaturated_fat_g value 3.2 sourced from USDA SR Legacy. All -1 values in per_100g represent nutrients not reported in the USDA SR Legacy entry for this product.
